memoryArtifacts.description:
Origin Displayed: English - Spelling variations of this family name include: Treadwell, Treadway, Threadwell, Tredway, Threadway, Tredway, Tradewell, Tradway and many more.
First found in Suffolk where they were anciently seated as Lords of the Manor. Some of the first settlers of this family name or some of its variants were: settlers who were recorded from the mid 17th century in the great migration from Europe. Migrants settled in the eastern seaboard from Newfoundland, to Maine, to Virginia, the Carolinas, and to the islands.
(From www.HouseOfNames.com Archives copyright  2000 - 2009). Recorded in various spelling forms including Treadwell, Tredwell, Tretwell and even Tradewell, this interesting name is Medieval English. It is of occupational origins, and was found mainly in the Eastern and Midland counties. As such it may describe either a dresser of cloth as shown below, or possibly a builder and maintainer of watering holes called "waella". Originally these were natural springs probably walled (dressed) to allow for the easy collection of fresh water, but later the description was used for deep wells, as we know them. Where the origin related top the textile trade, the work of the dresser was to scour and thicken the raw cloth by beating and trampling it in water, hence the term to "tread well". There are a variety of modern surnames associated with these processes involved in the wool and cloth trade. They are usually regional. For instance the name Fuller being found mostly in South East England and East Anglia, whilst Tucker is recorded mainly in the South West, and the even more popular Walker in West and Northern England. Treadwell geographically seems to come in the middle and east of the country. Early examples of the surname recordings include: Thomas Treddewel of the county of Suffolk, where he appears in the register of members of the Abbey of Bec in 1248, and Gabriell Treadwell who married Hope Cordey on 12 July 1686, at All Hallows church, London Wall, in the city of London. Surnames became necessary when governments introduced personal taxation. In England this was sometimes known as Poll Tax. Throughout the centuries, surnames in every country have continued to "develop" often leading to astonishing variants of the original spelling.  Copyright: Name Origin Research www.surnamedb.com 1980 - 2014.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Alexander Tredwell於1535年出生在英國英格蘭奧可福爾斯瓦爾可利福時，他的父親Alexander Tredwell33歲，母親Elizabeth Bird25歲。他於大約1559年與Dorothy Pomeroy在英國英格蘭奧可福爾斯瓦爾可利福結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1603年7月16日在他的家鄉享年68歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭奧可福爾傑維爾。

nameMeanings.name: Tredwell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Treadwell
meanings: English: metonymic occupational name from Middle English tred(de) ‘tread, walk, trample’ + wel(le) ‘well’, perhaps for a fuller or someone who trod grapes for wine production. Fulling was the process by which newly woven cloth was cleaned and shrunk by the use of heat, water, and pressure (from treading) before finally being stretched and laid out to dry on tenter hooks.

nameMeanings.name: Alexander
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Latin form of the Greek name Alexandros, from alexein ‘to defend’ + anēr ‘man, warrior’ (genitive andros). The name became extremely popular in the post-classical period, and was borne by several individuals in the New Testament and some early Christian saints. Its use as a common given name throughout Europe, however, derives largely from the fame of Alexander the Great , King of Macedon ( 356–323 bc ), around whom a large body of popular legend grew up in late antiquity, much of which came to be embodied in the medieval ‘Alexander romances’.

en.lifeSummary: When Alexander Tredwell was born in 1535, in Swalcliffe, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Alexander Tredwell, was 33 and his mother, Elizabeth Bird, was 25. He married Dorothy Pomeroy about 1559, in Swalcliffe, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 16 July 1603, in his hometown, at the age of 68, and was buried in Cherwell, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Alexander Tredwell est né en 1535, à Swalcliffe, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Alexander Tredwell, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Bird, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé Dorothy Pomeroy environ 1559, à Swalcliffe, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 16 juillet 1603, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 68 ans, et a été enterré à Cherwell, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ko.lifeSummary: Alexander Tredwell(이)가 1535년 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 스월c리프에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Alexander Tredwell(은)는 33세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Bird(은)는 25세였다. 그는 대략 1559년 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 스월c리프에서 Dorothy Pomeroy(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1603년 7월 16일 그의 고향에서 68세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 체웰에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Alexander Tredwell родился в 1535 г. в местности свалклифф, оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Alexander Tredwell было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Bird, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Dorothy Pomeroy около 1559 г. в местности свалклифф, оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 4 дочери. Он умер 16 июля 1603 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 68 лет и был похоронен в местности чирвелл, оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания.
